threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You call it a 'sandwich' and not a 'butty.' Also, you still think the buses are punctual.
Last night on the 196, a lad serenaded us with a ukulele cover of 'Wonderwall.' Must be the late-night Brixton spirit!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I realized I belonged when I saw the Turkish bakery open at 5 AM, the queue wrapped around the block, all chatting like it was just the morning greeting.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find an eclectic mix of vegan döner stands next to traditional currywurst shops, all packed with locals at 3 AM.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals don’t flinch at the sound of sirens; it’s the tourists jumping out of the way that give themselves away.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 47 at Civic Center, but it sat there for ages. Turns out a dog was chilling on the tracks, just enjoying the ride. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all bars serve tapas, but in Gràcia, many focus on craft cocktails. The vibe shifts dramatically once you step off the main streets.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always spot them by their five-gear bikes, struggling up the steep streets, while locals glide by on electric scooters.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot the newbies: they’re the ones trying to get on the bus at the wrong stop, oblivious to the four-minute walk to the train.
Caught the 68 last night; a guy tried to serenade the whole bus with an acoustic guitar. Only ended when someone threw a half-eaten burger at him.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The first time I saw a spontaneous street party outside my window, with kids and old folks dancing together. That’s when it clicked.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can grab a doner kebab at 4 AM after a night out, but only in Kreuzberg will they serve it with a side of techno beats from the club next door.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they know the taco truck schedules by heart but still hesitate at the BART closure drama.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo trying to woo a lady on the 31. She looked unimpressed, but he kept talking about how he knows 'the best view' in the city.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all bars serve paella. In Gràcia, it’s all about tapas and vermut. Good luck finding a decent paella here!

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones marveling at the bus driver’s route, still clutching that ancient metro map like a lifeline. You can see the confusion on their faces.
